questions.question: What is an 'Entity' in the context of modern search and AI?

options.text: A specific keyword you want to rank for.
A uniquely identifiable person, organization, or thing that the machine can track across the web.
A type of computer virus.
A ghostwriter who writes your blog posts.

questions.explanation: Search engines have moved from matching strings (keywords) to identifying things (entities). Building 'Entity Authority' means making sure the machine knows exactly who you are and why you are a trusted source.

questions.question: Which of the E-E-A-T letters is considered the most critical for overall search quality and safety?

options.text: Experience
Expertise
Authoritativeness
Trustworthiness

questions.explanation: While all factors are important, Trustworthiness is the ceiling. If a site is not trusted (due to security issues, lacks of transparency, or misleading info), high expertise or experience will not save its rankings.

questions.question: How does a solo founder build 'Topical Authority' effectively?

options.text: By writing 100 short posts about 100 different topics.
By writing a 'Cluster' of deep, interrelated content (20+ articles) that covers every angle of a specific niche topic.
By buying backlinks from news websites.
By changing their name to match a popular keyword.

questions.explanation: Topical Authority is earned through depth. When an AI sees that you have covered a specific problem from every possible angle, it identifies your site as the definitive source for that entity.

questions.question: What is 'Disambiguation' and why is it important for your personal brand?

options.text: It means excluding competitors from your search results.
It is the process of ensuring that your name and identity are consistent across the web so AI models don't confuse you with others.
It is a technical way to hide your location from Google.
It means using complex language to sound more intelligent.

questions.explanation: If your identity is fragmented across different names or handles, AI assistants might fail to give you credit for all your contributions. Consistency helps the machine build a complete profile of your 'Entity.'

questions.question: A solo founder shares a detailed story of a major mistake they made and how they fixed it. Which part of E-E-A-T does this primarily build?

options.text: Experience (First-hand, personal proof)
Authoritativeness (Being the go-to source)
Disambiguation
Schema Markup

questions.explanation: First-hand 'Experience' is the newest part of E-E-A-T. AI models prioritize content that shows the writer actually 'did the work' rather than just summarizing it. Sharing mistakes is a high-signal indicator of experience.

questions.question: Look at your primary acquisition channel (e.g., your blog or LinkedIn). If I were to remove your name and logo, would a reader still know this content came from a genuine practitioner in your niche? What 'Experience' or 'Expertise' signals could you add to your next 3 posts to make your 'Entity' undeniable?
promptHints: Include more specific data or metrics.
Reference a recent client conversation or problem.
Share a specific decision and the 'Why' behind it.
